narrative.summary: Brand-new breakfast sandwich shop, open 2 weeks, already has evangelical fanbase and lines. Middle Eastern-inflected sandwiches, just steps from Bathurst station. 4.8★ (60 reviews). Tue-Sun 9am-3pm. Worth a deliberate stop for John’s design/architecture/art/food radar.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're steps from Bathurst station, mid-Annex, in a small takeout counter that's only been open two weeks but already draws morning lines. The owner works behind a tight galley setup where you can watch him press dough and crack eggs — this is counter service, a few stools maybe, the kind of place where the architecture is the ritual of assembly. It's spare and functional, all attention on the flatbread baking in real time.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: Palestinian breakfast sandwiches built on flatbread that gets baked fresh for every order — you're not eating yesterday's pita. The za'atar & egg and foole & egg are the early hits, Middle Eastern pantry staples layered warm and runny like a flatbread pizza that folds. There are solid veggie and vegan options, which is rare for breakfast sandwiches that actually satisfy, and everything hovers around $8-12. The menu is tight, the flavour is not.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: You're catching the subway and want something better than a bodega egg sandwich, or you're building a Saturday morning around Annex errands and need fuel that won't weigh you down. Get there before noon on weekends unless you like standing in lines.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Delicious new breakfast spot we stumbled into this morning. Our group got the za’atar & egg, foole & egg, and the egg & cheese and they were all a 10/10. Super flavourful and hearty. The owner is extremely sweet and personable. Will definitely be back!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "My new favourite sandwich shop! Love the veggie/vegan friendly options (so rare to find a good veggie breakfast!! And at such a great price??) The owner is so sweet and kind, and clearly has put a lot of love into the space and the food he makes. PLUS THE BREAD IS BAKED FRESH EVERY SANDWICH.  Can’t wait to go back!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Takeout shop across from Bathurst subway station that sells Palestinian inspired breakfast sandwiches. The sandwiches are assembled like a flatbread pizza with the bread baked fresh at the time of ordering (takes about 2 mins) and then folded over into a sandwich. The smell of freshly baked bread fills the air, wrapping itself around you like a mother’s warm embrace.

I got the egg and cheese sandwich, which had arugula and some sort of green sauce on it. The flavours were fresh and bright and delicious. I finished the sandwich feeling full, but not bloated or ashamed.

Guy who runs the place was friendly and seemed to take a lot of pride in his shop, which was clean and nicely decorated. Makann is the kind of independent shop Toronto needs more of, and which feels good supporting."
